message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We aim to study the relationship between occupation distribution within each county and COVID-19 cumulative incidence and vaccination rate in the United States.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We collected county-level data from January 22, 2020 up to December 25, 2021. We fit multivariate linear models to find the relationship of the percentage of people employed by 23 main occupations.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Counties with more health-related jobs, office support roles, community service, sales, production and material moving occupations had higher COVID-19 cumulative incidence. During the uptick of the “Delta” COVID variant (stratified period July 1-Dec 25), counties with more transportation occupations had significantly more COVID-19 cumulative incidence than before.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Significance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Understanding the association between occupations and COVID-19 cumulative incidence on an ecological level can provide information for precision public health strategies for prevention and protecting vulnerable workers.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Impact Statement</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We used data from US Census and COVID-19 data to explore the association between occupations and COVID-19 cumulative incidence and vaccination rate on an ecological level, which can provide information for precision public health strategies for prevention of spread of disease and protecting vulnerable workers.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Occupational determinants of COVID-19 cases and vaccination: an ecological analysis of counties in the United States as of December 2021
